headline: PulteGroup (PHM) Posted Weaker Q2 Earnings, Is The Stock Still Undervalued?
summary: How PulteGroup’s Latest Earnings Shift The Conversation PulteGroup (PHM) is back in focus after its second quarter earnings showed revenue of US$3,982.96 million and net income of US$472 million, both lower than the same period a year ago. For investors, the weaker earnings and basic EPS of US$2.49, compared with US$3.05 a year earlier, set the backdrop for reassessing what the current share price reflects about PulteGroup’s homebuilding and financial services operations. headline: PulteGroup Ekes Out an Earnings Beat, and Juices the Stock
summary: PulteGroup  barely topped earnings expectations on Wednesday, but did enough to convince Wall Street that the home construction business is turning the corner.  Pulte posted second-quarter earnings of $2.48 a share, way off the $3.03 a year ago.  Wall Street’s projection was $2.36.

headline: PulteGroup (NYSE:PHM) Beats Q2 CY2026 Sales Expectations
summary: Homebuilding company PulteGroup (NYSE:PHM) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, but sales fell by 9.6% year on year to $3.98 billion. Its GAAP profit of $2.48 per share was 5.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: PulteGroup Reports Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results
summary: ATLANTA, July 22, 2026--PulteGroup, Inc. (NYSE: PHM) announced today financial results for its second quarter ended June 30, 2026. For the quarter, the Company reported net income of $472 million, or $2.48 per share. In the prior year period, the Company reported net income of $608 million, or $3.03 per share.
